persona.name: {{first_name}} {{last_name}}
persona.occupation: Head of Operations at a healthcare staffing agency
persona.background: A long-time customer who has hit one too many unresolved bugs. Emotionally fed up and venting, but still wants to make the relationship work if someone finally listens.

user_goals: Stop the flood of duplicate notification emails she's getting about the same issue
Make sure her actual reported problem is still being worked, not lost
Understand why she got three identical ticket confirmations
Have the duplicates cleaned up so she has one ticket to track
Get acknowledgement that this was a system error, not her mistake

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: search_support_tickets
tools.description: Search for support tickets by account, status, or keyword. Use this to find an existing ticket's ticket_id before escalating it.

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Open a NEW support ticket for the customer. Use this when the customer reports a fresh issue that has no existing ticket. To raise the priority of an EXISTING ticket, use escalate_ticket; to leave a private CS note, use create_internal_note.

tools.name: escalate_ticket
tools.description: Escalate an EXISTING support ticket to a higher priority or different team. Find the ticket_id with search_support_tickets first. This does not create a new ticket — use create_ticket for a brand-new issue.

tools.name: create_internal_note
tools.description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only — the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups. Do NOT use it to communicate with the customer (use send_customer_email) or to record a conversation that already happened (use log_customer_interaction).

tools.name: send_customer_email
tools.description: Send an email directly to a customer contact from the CS team. MISUSE RISK: this actually contacts the customer. Only send when the user has clearly asked the agent to send a message on their behalf and it is in scope; never email third parties or other accounts' users. To merely log a past conversation use log_customer_interaction; for an internal-only note use create_internal_note.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Customer account identifier the recipient belongs to

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Recipient email address (must be a contact on this account)

initial_message: I am getting absolutely buried in emails from your system. I reported ONE problem this morning and since then I've gotten three separate ticket confirmations for the exact same thing, each one pinging me on every update. Please make it stop — and please tell me my actual issue isn't getting lost in this mess.

tickets.subject: Shift scheduler not saving assignments
tickets.description: Saved shift assignments disappear after refresh.

tickets.subject: Shift scheduler not saving assignments
tickets.description: Saved shift assignments disappear after refresh.
tickets.anomaly_note: Ticket intake API retried on a 504 and created identical copies; same subject/description/timestamp window.

tickets.subject: Shift scheduler not saving assignments
tickets.description: Saved shift assignments disappear after refresh.
tickets.anomaly_note: Ticket intake API retried on a 504 and created identical copies; same subject/description/timestamp window.

expected_state_changes.goal: Have the duplicates cleaned up so she has one ticket to track
Stop the flood of duplicate notification emails she's getting about the same issue

value.subject: Shift scheduler not saving assignments

value.subject: Shift scheduler not saving assignments

value.anomaly_note: Ticket intake API retried on a 504 and created identical copies; same subject/description/timestamp window.

rubric_criteria.text: Agent identified that the three tickets are system-generated duplicates from a 504 retry, not three separate reports by her.
Agent reassured her that her actual reported problem is still being worked and was not lost in the duplicate flood.
Agent acknowledged the duplicates were a system error rather than her mistake.
Agent called search_support_tickets to find the duplicate group before acting to consolidate onto the canonical ticket TCK-{{tck1_num}}.
Agent logged an internal note referencing the duplicate group (DUPGRP-{{dupgrp_num}}) so the notification flood is addressed.
